introParagraphs: This accessibility statement explains how we design and maintain olympia-2026-webapp for all users.
Our goal is to provide clear, navigable, and inclusive digital experiences across devices.

sections.heading: 1. Scope of this statement
paragraphs: This statement applies to the olympia-2026-webapp website and its public pages.
It covers essential user journeys such as navigation, language switching, country and sport results, and legal pages.

sections.heading: 2. Accessibility standards
paragraphs: The website is developed against WCAG 2.2 Level AA principles.
We also follow semantic HTML and ARIA best practices to support assistive technologies.
list: Keyboard operability for interactive controls
Sufficient contrast and readable typography
Accessible names for buttons, links, and tables
Responsive layouts for desktop and mobile use

sections.heading: 3. Known limitations
paragraphs: Some third-party content and externally embedded media may not fully meet the same accessibility level.
We continuously review and improve these areas where technically possible.
list: Third-party widgets and embeds
External downloadable documents
Media provided by external partners

sections.heading: 4. Feedback and support
paragraphs: If you encounter an accessibility barrier, please contact our support channel.
Include the page URL and a short description so we can investigate quickly.

introParagraphs: This Cookie Policy explains how cookies and similar technologies are used on this website.
It helps you understand which categories we use and how to manage your preferences.

sections.heading: 1. What are cookies?
paragraphs: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website.
They can be session-based (deleted after your visit) or persistent (kept for a defined period).

sections.heading: 2. Cookie categories we use
list: Strictly necessary cookies for core functionality
Functional cookies for preferences and usability
Performance cookies for analytics and quality improvements
Marketing cookies for personalised communication where applicable

sections.heading: 3. Managing cookies
paragraphs: You can manage your preferences in browser settings and in our cookie controls where available.
Blocking some categories may affect parts of the website experience.

sections.heading: 4. More information
paragraphs: For details on personal data processing, please also read our Privacy Policy.
For cookie background information, visit www.allaboutcookies.org.

introParagraphs: This Privacy Policy describes how personal data is processed when you use this website and related services.
It applies to account, navigation, and service interactions relevant to olympia-2026-webapp.

sections.heading: 1. Who we are
paragraphs: The service is provided in the context of Olympic digital information services.
Data responsibilities may involve trusted partners where indicated in specific notices.

sections.heading: 2. Data we collect
list: Information you provide directly (for example account data)
Technical data such as browser, device, and usage logs
Preference and language settings
Cookie-related identifiers where consent applies

sections.heading: 3. Why we process data
list: Provide and secure the service
Improve quality and user experience
Support communication and user requests
Meet legal and compliance obligations

sections.heading: 4. Your rights
paragraphs: Depending on applicable law, you may request access, correction, deletion, restriction, objection, or portability.
You may also withdraw consent where processing is based on consent.

sections.heading: 5. Contact
paragraphs: For privacy requests, use our official support and data protection channels.

footerNotes: International Olympic Committee, Maison Olympique (Olympic House), 1007 Lausanne, Switzerland

introParagraphs: These Terms of Service govern your use of olympia-2026-webapp and related digital features.
By using the website, you agree to these terms and to applicable legal requirements.

sections.heading: 1. Use of the service
paragraphs: The website is provided for information and interactive Olympic-related content.
Certain features may require an account or may be restricted by role.

sections.heading: 2. Acceptable behavior
list: Use the service lawfully and respectfully
Do not misuse, disrupt, scrape, or reverse engineer the platform
Do not upload unlawful, harmful, or rights-infringing content
Do not attempt unauthorised access to systems or data

sections.heading: 3. Intellectual property
paragraphs: Content, branding, and platform elements are protected by intellectual property laws.
No rights are granted beyond normal personal use unless explicitly stated.

sections.heading: 4. Service availability and liability
paragraphs: We aim for reliable operation but cannot guarantee uninterrupted availability.
To the extent allowed by law, liability is limited for indirect or consequential damages.

sections.heading: 5. Changes and governing law
paragraphs: We may update these terms and publish the latest version on this page.
Applicable law and jurisdiction are determined by the legal notices provided for this service.
